title: SOC 2 Compliance Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on Trust Services Criteria, logging, and change management.

questions.question: What is the primary business reason for a SaaS company to undergo a SOC 2 audit?

options.text: To guarantee their software is completely bug-free.
To prove to enterprise clients that their infrastructure, data handling, and internal company processes are secure and trustworthy, unlocking massive enterprise sales.
Because it is legally required by the United States Federal Government for all websites.
To automatically patch vulnerabilities in the operating system.

questions.question: Which of the five SOC 2 'Trust Services Criteria' is strictly mandatory for all companies undergoing the audit?

options.text: Privacy
Security
Processing Integrity
Availability

questions.question: During a SOC 2 audit, you are asked who has direct administrative access to the production databases. What is the most compliant and secure answer?

options.text: All developers, to ensure they can debug issues quickly.
Access is completely disabled for everyone, including pipeline robots; the database is immutable.
Only the automated CI/CD pipeline service accounts and a very restricted number of senior engineers using monitored, temporary 'break-glass' credentials.
Only the CEO and the Legal team.

questions.question: How do DevOps teams prove to a SOC 2 auditor that 'Change Management' controls are strictly enforced?

options.text: By demonstrating that the CI/CD pipeline and GitHub repository utilize strict branch protection, preventing unreviewed code from ever reaching the production branch.
By showing them screenshots of the developers promising not to break things.
By deleting commit logs older than 30 days to hide undocumented changes.
By allowing developers to bypass Git and upload files straight via FTP.

questions.question: Which AWS capability is considered absolutely essential to proving compliance regarding 'Monitoring & Logging' to a SOC 2 auditor?

options.text: Amazon Lex
AWS CloudTrail, because it immutably logs every single API call and action taken by any user or service across the entire AWS account history.
AWS CodeDeploy
Amazon SQS
